parsed_review.summary: CP00-823 (Platform Extensibility "Fixture Service Bridge", Risk C, 150 units, RP27.P01.M06.S10-RP27.P02.M03.S16) is a descriptor-only pack and its claims are supported by the source I inspected via Read/Grep/Glob. The manifest declares production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, implementation_layer:"descriptor", descriptor_only:true, writes_product_state:false, no_real_data:true, and production_ready_descriptor_verified:false, so there is no production/runtime overclaim. registry.js/validators.js build PlatformExtensibilityCp823FixtureServiceBridgeDescriptor (source_descriptor CP822) with a comprehensive no-write attestation; the CP823 validator enforces descriptor_only, runtime_execution:false, no API-key/webhook/workflow payload secrets, no real client data, no permission/audit/product-state writes, no runtime opening (api_key_issued/webhook_delivered/workflow_executed all false), Hermes gate H27 with emits_runtime_receipt:false, Claude gate C27 read_only:true promotes_final_approval:false, and handoff to CP00-824/RP27.P02.M03.S17. Coverage requirements sum to 150 and the per-deliverable counts match the prompt distribution; model.test.js asserts zero validation errors for CP823 binding, coverage, descriptor, and contract projection. The committed 584KB contract has latest_pack_id CP00-823, validation.valid:true, and an all-false closing no_write_attestation. Models are tenant-scoped, matter-trace-required, and AuditEvent-referenced. Review hardening checks pass: auth preflight exit 0 with 1226-byte valid JSON, no forbidden tools, schema present, no omitted active untracked implementation files, and no 0-byte/malformed/tool-call-shaped evidence. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approver, and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review packet/receipt handling, not product runtime. Two non-blocking P3 boundary notes: (1) downstream closeout artifacts (command-evidence.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, claude-review-result.json) are not yet present and, as a read-only reviewer without Bash, I cannot independently execute the 10 validation commands, so this PASS is conditioned on the deterministic closeout producing passing command-evidence and on human final approval before any runtime/enterprise-trust opening; (2) the production_ready_flag string label could be misread as an assertion while production_ready is false. blocks_goal_closeout is false because the pack does not claim runtime or enterprise trust; that authority boundary is acknowledged as external to this receipt.

findings.title: Downstream closeout artifacts pending; validation commands not independently executable by read-only reviewer
findings.details: required_pack_artifacts lists manifest.json, command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but docs/closeout-packs/cp00-823/ currently contains only manifest.json (the closed pack CP00-822 has all five plus raw-output.json/review-receipt.json in artifacts/). This is the expected pre-closeout state: this Claude review precedes adjudication/inspection and produces claude-review-result.json downstream. However, because the review is read-only with no Bash, I cannot independently execute the 10 expected validation commands (npm test, npm run validate, weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane validate, git diff --check, etc.); I verified the implementation, validator logic, tests, and contract by inspection only. The manifest also still shows status:in_progress and production_ready:false/production_ready_descriptor_verified:false, so no claim is overstated at this point.
findings.recommendation: Before flipping production_ready and committing, ensure the deterministic closeout pipeline produces command-evidence.json capturing passing results for all 10 validation commands, plus adjudication.md and construction-inspection.json, and that human final approval is recorded prior to any runtime/enterprise-trust opening. Treat this PASS as conditioned on that command-evidence.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking. Expected pre-closeout state confirmed against CP00-822 pattern; does not block pack or goal closeout. Surfaced to bound the scope of a read-only review.

findings.title: production_ready_flag label may read as an assertion while production_ready is false
findings.details: production_ready_flag is set to "platform_extensibility_fixture_service_bridge_descriptor_verified" while production_ready:false and implementation_summary.production_ready_descriptor_verified:false. The adjacent _descriptor_verified:false and top-level production_ready:false make the true state unambiguous, and the *_flag string is a stable per-pack identifier (consistent with CP820-CP822 bindings in registry.js), not an assertion of readiness. A future reader skimming only the flag name could nonetheless misinterpret it as a verified claim.
findings.recommendation: Optionally clarify (naming or a comment) that production_ready_flag is a descriptor label, not a readiness assertion, and that production_ready/production_ready_descriptor_verified are authoritative. No code or data change required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking readability nit. State is internally disambiguated by production_ready:false and production_ready_descriptor_verified:false.
